title: Macrame plant hanger
subtitle: A knotted cotton cord hanger for a 10-15 cm plant pot using square knots and a gathering finish
excerpt: This plant hanger uses eight lengths of cotton macrame cord folded over a metal ring at the top and knotted into a square-knot spiral column, an alternating-knot basket, and a gathered base. It fits a standard 10-15 cm plant pot and hangs approximately 60-70 cm from the ring to the bottom of the fringe. The knots are few and the pattern straightforward.
sourceNotes: Square-knot plant hangers are the standard entry project for macrame. Method is observation-led and consistent with contemporary macrame instruction.

content.text: The hanger has three sections. A knotted column below the ring. An
basket that holds the pot. And a
at the base that pulls all cords together before the fringe.

items.name: Metal or wooden ring, 5-7 cm diameter

content.text: Step 1: Cut and mount the cords

Cut 8 cords, each 8 m long. Fold each cord in half and attach it to the metal ring using a lark's head knot: fold the cord in half, push the folded loop through the ring from below, bring the two tails over the ring and through the loop, pull tight. Repeat for all 8 cords. You will have 16 working cords hanging from the ring.

Divide the 16 cords into 4 groups of 4. In each group, work a column of square knots: the outer 2 cords are the working cords and the inner 2 are the filler cords. Work 5-6 square knots per group to create a 10-12 cm column. All four columns hang side by side below the ring.

Leave a gap of 5-6 cm below the columns (this forms the sides of the basket). Then work one row of alternating square knots: take 2 cords from the right of one column and 2 cords from the left of the adjacent column and tie a single square knot. Repeat around all four groups. Leave another 5-6 cm gap. Work a second row of alternating square knots in the same way.

Bring all 16 cords together into a single bundle. Cut a separate 60 cm length of cord. Hold the bundle tightly and wind the separate cord around it 8-10 times, starting 3 cm below the last alternating row. Thread both ends of the wrapping cord through the centre of the coil using a blunt darning needle, pull tight, and trim. Trim the fringe below the gathering knot to an even length of 15-20 cm.

items.fix: Before tying the alternating rows, place the pot in position and adjust the gap so the knots sit at the widest point of the pot. A gap of 4-5 cm works for a 10 cm pot; 6-7 cm for a 15 cm pot.
items.cause: Gap between the column and the alternating rows not matched to the pot diameter
items.problem: The basket section is too wide or too narrow for the pot

items.fix: For a flat square knot, the first half-knot goes left-over-right, and the second goes right-over-left. Reversing produces a spiral (half-hitch column), which is a different, also valid look.
items.cause: All square knots tied in the same direction rather than alternating left-over-right and right-over-left
items.problem: The square knot columns twist into a spiral instead of lying flat

items.fix: Thread both ends of the wrapping cord through the coil from opposite ends, pull both tight, then trim the tails close to the coil.
items.cause: Wrapping cord not threaded through the coil before trimming
items.problem: The gathering knot slides or unravels
